steps.title: Count the data values
steps.content: The dataset has 9 values: 71, 72, 73, 76, 77, 79, 83, 87, 93. They are already arranged in ascending order.

steps.title: Find the median using the middle value rule
steps.content:
When a dataset has an odd number of values, the median is the middle value. With 9 values, the position of the median is:
$$\text{Position} = \frac{9 + 1}{2} = 5$$
Counting to the 5th position: 71 (1st), 72 (2nd), 73 (3rd), 76 (4th), 77 (5th). The median is 77. That matches choice B — B is correct.
